item.title: Forever Forward Update
item.description: The site is now updated for the new season, Forever Forward! The move updates are just about all as expected, except for Dragon Ascent being reduced to 45 energy. If you were using the preview site over the past week, the rankings should look just about the same. Have fun trying out new Pokemon and good luck to start the season!

item.title: Forever Forward Rankings Preview
item.description:
A preview is now available for next season, Forever Forward! Note that some updated move parameters are speculative, so they may change when the official numbers become official when the season launches.

Next season's changes should have limited impact on the top meta, but rounds the middle with a number of new contenders. Kanto Ninetales, Jumpluff, Seismitoad, and even Sliggoo all look to find a spot in the open league. With Wigglytuff nerfed, Sylveon and Aromatisse also emerge as new Fairies to take its place. Take a look and see you when the season starts!

item.title: Oinkologne Rankings
item.description: Lechonk Community Day gives Oinkologne access to Mud Slap! Even after the nerf, this boosts Oinkologne to a comparable tier as Furret, Dunsparce, or Greedent. Its strong Fast Move pressure separates it from similar Normal types, which tend to be energy oriented. Female Oinkologne has better stats than male, so keep this in mind during Community Day!

item.title: Shareable Links for Matrix Battle
item.description: The Matrix Battle feature now supports shareable links, limited up to 16 custom Pokemon. You can use this for comparing teams in team tournaments (up to 8v8) or comparing matchups against a preselected group (such as examining IV combinations). Matrix Battles which feature more than 16 custom Pokemon are still not shareable but may be supported in the future.

item.title: New Shadow Rankings
item.description: A handful of new Shadow Pokemon will be making their debut alongside Shadow Landorus. Of the bunch, only Araquanid draws much PvP interest while Bewear, Shiinotic, and Heliolisk are better for the spice cabinet. Keep an eye out for any exciting additions to Rocket Grunt and Leader lineups, and don't forget to remove Frustration while you can!

item.title: Orthworm Rankings
item.description: Orthworm is making its raid-exclusive debut, and it's a solid pickup for PvP. It has pure Steel typing, decent bulk, and EdgeQuake coverage with access to non-STAB Mud Slap, Rock Tomb, and Earthquake. Iron Head is also an option but less appealing into other Steels. Its prospects look best in themed cups such as Fantasy Cup, which returns alongside it. We'll see if it worms its way into any metas!

item.title: Great League Top Performers
item.description:
I've updated the Top Performers page with the latest Great League training data, including early Tinkaton performance. There's no denying this Pokemon is a meta changer, even as hard counters like Jellicent and Forretress are out for the Fairy hunt. Tinkaton counts among most of the top performing teams, where it's typically paired with Feraligatr.

These two are performing so well, in fact, that other Pokemon are struggling in comparison. A few that are keeping their head above water are Galarian Corsola, Empoleon, and Forretress. On the other hand, Shadow Drapion, Annihilape, and Wigglytuff have taken big performance hits.

Good luck as the meta adapts to this new menace!

item.title: NAIC Championship Cup Rankings
item.description: Rankings are now available for the NAIC Championship Series Cup, which will be the featured meta of NAIC 2026. Note that NAIC will take place shortly after a seasonal move update, so keep in mind during your preparation that the meta may be impacted as a result. Good luck practicing and studying!

item.title: Tinkaton Rankings
item.description: Break out your hammers! Tinkaton is receiving its exclusive move this upcoming Community Day, and it looks to become a smash hit in Great and Ultra League. It comes at a steep 60 energy and with Steel offensive typing, but its sheer base power (subject to change) makes it impossible to ignore. Not only does Tinkaton improve its neutral matchups, especially as a closer, Gigaton Hammer gives it a major edge against rival Fairies. This one should be a must have for PvP!

item.title: Mimikyu Mechanics
item.description:
Mimikyu has been released with a new battle form mechanic. It's currently only selectable in Rocket Battles, but while it waits for its PvP debut, I've updated the simulations with its form change mechanics observed so far. Here's a rundown on how Mimikyu and its Busted Form work:

- Mimikyu starts each battle in its regular form.
- The first time Mimikyu takes an unshielded Charged Attack, it blocks it just like a shield.
- Mimikyu then enters its Busted Form and receives a 1 stage drop to its Defense (equivalent to one Sand Tomb or Poison Fang).
- Mimikyu's Busted Form and its Defense drop last the rest of the battle, even after switching out.

This effectively gives Mimikyu shield advantage in all of its matchups. While the Defense drop provides a downside, Mimikyu can use shields to delay that Defense drop if desired. Conversely, its opponents will be incentivized to throw their cheapest Charged Attacks, even not very effective ones, to break Mimikyu's disguise as early as possible. At first glance, this gives Mimikyu a significant advantage in its 1v1 matchups. However, note that the sims won't fully represent the effects of the Defense drop across a whole match.

Mimikyu's mechanics are subject to change, so consider the sims as a preview only. In the meantime, it looks like a great Pokemon to hunt and prepare for while it's available.

item.title: Fantasy Cup Rankings
item.description: Fantasy Cup makes a comeback, and so do a few Pokemon on the meta list. Flygon is revamped and ready to roll with an all-new moveset, Alolan Ninetales and Sandslash benefit from a buff to Powder Snow, and Primarina looks to share the spotlight with Azumarill thanks to the improved viability of Waterfall. This cup hinges on key anti-meta picks lower in the rankings, such as the Grass-types Ferrothorn and Shiinotic. Still, it could be the tried and true combo of Galarian Stunfisk and Azumarill that stand the wheel of time.

item.title: Electric Cup Rankings
item.description: Electric Cup returns in just over a week, and it's no shock that this format will remain a small one. This time, Hidden Power Luxray might be reigned in by Galvantula and Hisuian Electrode, who look to be among the cup's strongest contenders. Since its last appearance, attacks like Swift, Low Kick, and Acid have seen wider distribution, adding more offensive options throughout the cup. While this may help round out some teams, look for the action to center around Galvantula, Hisuian Electrode, and their hardest available counters.

item.title: Jungle Cup Rankings
item.description: Jungle Cup returns, but this tropical format is undergoing an ice age! Piloswine and Articuno look to be strong contenders with the buff to Powder Snow. Forretress will be a key Pokemon to protect frontrunners like Altaria and Diggersby. Jungle Cup is typically a more open meta, and this time Galarian Stunfisk and Gliscor also look to join the fray. This might be a chance to put new Great League Pokemon to the test. Good luck in the jungle!

item.title: Cinderace Rankings
item.description: Fire types tend to struggle in PvP, and Cinderace is no exception even with two new moves for its Community Day. On paper, both Blast Burn and Pyro Ball are excellent attacks, but Cinderace's stats are made of paper, too, so it needs a bit more help. Talonflame and Ninetales are more likely to see the field until Cinderace gets a better Fast Attack like Ember, or access to meaningful coverage. As always, look to catch and ready a few just in case!

item.title: Spring Cup Rankings
item.description: Spring Cup blooms again with its small triangle of Water, Grass, and Fairy. Naturally, the best Pokemon in this cup tend to have Steel or Poison typing to pack on resistances, or non-STAB attacks to break the mold. Empoleon, Ferrothorn, and Jellicent are top threats to watch, while Pokemon like Mawile and Gourgeist look to slot into unique roles. This meta should be top-heavy, but its roster is just deep enough to provide interesting core breakers and moveset surprises.

channel.title: PvPoke
channel.description: Website updates for PvPoke.com and other news for Pokemon GO PvP.
